title: hosts — kyzo-lsp split + lsp_api door death + kyzo-core/tests preserve-in-place — cut destiny under decisions.md
privilege: Architect cut contract only. decisions.md is sole Spec authority — this file yields on conflict. Claim-tag inflation or jointly impossible meters void this plan. The kyzo-bin server split (feeds.rs/console.rs) is already executed in the tree; this instance seals its remaining ledger notes and rules the LSP.

scope.this_cut: Living targets: kyzo-lsp/src/{main,translate}.rs (translate.rs is a 1-line stub; main.rs 837 lines still monolithic); kyzo-core/src/lib.rs (the lsp_api door dies); crates/kyzo-core/tests/ preserved in place as the sealed contract's integration suite; kyzo-bin/src/server/mod.rs ledger notes sealed.
scope.not_this_json: Everything engine-side (01–05, 07). The kyzo-bin door renames (client.rs→repl/fetch.rs, relations.rs→bulk.rs, changes+standing→feeds.rs, pages→console.rs) are executed history.
foundation_already_true: kyzo-bin/src/server/{feeds,console}.rs exist with content; old changes/standing/pages files are gone
kyzo-lsp/src/translate.rs exists as a stub; main.rs still holds transport + translation + features
kyzo-core/src/lib.rs still exports lsp_api (rg hit) — the door is alive and condemned
05-data-rules-peels.json seats fixed-rule name+arity declarations in kyzo-model (the vocabulary this cut's LSP rewire needs)
scope.target_not_current: by_target and allowlist.targets are target-tree paths only.

authority: docs/deprecated/deprecated-migrated.md — kyzo-lsp/main.rs, kyzo-bin/server/mod.rs, crates/kyzo-core/tests/ census entries
This file — cut destiny; subordinate to decisions.md

claim_tags.law: Mandatory Spec tags from decisions.md only. Every unrepresentable re-tags under exactly one. Inflating Unexposed to Unconstructible voids this plan.
claim_tags.Unconstructible: No path through the sealed Rust type and crate boundary constructs the illegal state. Proof: private constructor + compile-fail a crate-consumer trybuild can demonstrate.
claim_tags.Refused: Reachable program; typed runtime outcome. Named refuse variants.
claim_tags.Unexposed: Absent from the public API but reachable by a hostile adapter. Name residual risk; real detection or residual accepted.

joint_satisfaction.law: Allowlist ⊇ every TARGET file the meters require to change. No condemned file paths this cut; the condemned surface is the lsp_api symbol door, closed by grep-delete.
joint_satisfaction.proof_pairing: grep-delete for the lsp_api door; the sealed-door boundary is proven by the existing public-surface suite.
joint_satisfaction.no_condemned_rewire: No path-level condemnation this cut; symbol-level deletes only.

deletes.law: Symbol-level E-actions; no file dies this cut.

symbols.name: kyzo::lsp_api (check_script door in kyzo-core/src/lib.rs)
symbols.why: The LSP speaks kyzo-model's public parse surface; a core-crate door minted for one host is a second meaning door on the sealed facade. Dies; validation rewires to kyzo-model.

delete_guarded_invariants.name: no lsp_api door on the sealed facade
LSP does not import the engine
delete_guarded_invariants.close: rg 'kyzo_core|kyzo-core' crates/kyzo-lsp/Cargo.toml crates/kyzo-lsp/src → zero after the catalog-door qae is executed (until then: exactly the sanctioned sealed-door usage, no engine-internal paths)

not_deletes.why: PRESERVE-IN-PLACE: external integration crates reach only the public kyzo:: facade — the exact boundary the target seals; the suite is the drift detector for the facade's re-export decision.

not_deletes.item: kyzo-bin server/mod.rs dropped-not-stubbed ledger + body-limit DoS fix
not_deletes.why: The richest behavior-change ledger in the crate; each upstream feature's absence is argued from a contract with the fix's real seat named. Travels with the file, sealed by this instance's notes.
Earned its own narrow copy — no access to the crate-internal scanner; same class as reject_excessive_nesting for the identical reason. Drift-honest hand copy with blast radius stated.

meters: rg -n 'fn diagnostics_from_report|struct LineIndex' crates/kyzo-lsp/src/translate.rs → definitions present
rg -n 'struct LineIndex' crates/kyzo-lsp/src/main.rs → zero (moved, not twinned)

refused.law: Typed runtime outcomes. Named variants. Authority honest.

items.name: HTTP request body past the per-route limit
items.variant: 413-class body-limit refusal (only /import raises its cap via --max-import-body-mb)
items.why: Upstream disabled the 2 MiB cap router-wide — a one-connection memory-exhaustion DoS; the per-route fix is sealed doctrine.

items.name: malformed bind/port config at server startup
items.variant: typed config refusal (lifting the startup unwraps per the zone note)
items.why: Process-entry failures are lawful panics under the zone's no-panic-escapes-a-HANDLER clause, but the map's 'malformed config is a typed refusal' line wants bind/port parse lifted to a typed refusal on arrival — recorded as the seat's next edit.

unexposed.law: Residual hostile-adapter risk with real detection or residual accepted.

items.name: LSP client sending positions past the end of the line/document
items.residual_risk: Hostile or buggy editors send over-long UTF-16 positions; a naive index would panic the server.
items.detection: clamp-never-crash posture is the translation tier's law (position CLAMPS past-the-end; a clamp is a wrong-looking diagnostic, never a crashed server); the LSP smoke suite exercises the clamp; residual accepted for wrong-looking columns

carried_obligations.obligation: AGGREGATIONS/KEYWORDS consts are drift-honest hand copies (three hand-kept lists: parse's, the LSP's, the grammar's keywords — one concept each). When 05's model vocabulary cut lands (aggregate names, fixed-rule name+arity), the LSP consumes kyzo-model and the hand lists die.
zone-lsp's formatter clause is honored today only by absence — when formatting lands it must call kyzo-model's format.rs (the one canonical pretty-printer), never a local one.

tests.law: The external suite is the facade's drift detector; host tests stay host-side.

fate.to: crates/kyzo-core/tests/ — model-vocabulary re-exports resolve per lib.rs's facade re-export; no test text changes if the facade keeps the names

meters: crates/kyzo-core/tests/public_api_surface.rs still compiles against the sealed facade after the lsp_api deletion

authoritative_constructs: LineIndex (UTF-16 position law) + word_at + diagnostics_from_report + collect_labels — kyzo-lsp/src/translate.rs
wire transport (read_message/write_message) + server loop + feature handlers (hover, completion, definition) + catalog tier — kyzo-lsp/src/main.rs

unconstructible.name: a host reaching engine internals past the sealed facade
unconstructible.proof: compile-fail: kyzo-core internals are pub(crate); a host crate consuming anything not re-exported by lib.rs fails to resolve — the public_api_surface suite locks the export set

rulings.a: The diagnostics translation tier (LineIndex, word_at, diagnostics_from_report, collect_labels) moves whole to kyzo-lsp/src/translate.rs — 'miette → LSP: spans to ranges, errors to diagnostics' is that seat's substance. Transport, loop, and features stay main.rs. No twinning: LineIndex defined once.

Validation rewires from kyzo::lsp_api::check_script to kyzo-model's public parse surface. The language server needs the language; the parse surface is the language's door. The lsp_api facade door dies with no replacement on kyzo-core.

crates/kyzo-core/tests/ remains the sealed contract's own integration suite. The one cross-cutting rewire is transparent: the facade re-exports kyzo-model vocabulary (DataValue, Tuple, Validity, UuidWrapper, …); if the facade keeps the names, no test text changes — and if it breaks a consumer, this suite breaks first, at the contract. That is its job.

kyzo-bin server/mod.rs's dropped-not-stubbed ledger (no per-request mutability override — mutability read off the parsed program; no /transact — the fix is runtime-tier API, not a bin workaround; no /import-from-backup — restore is whole-store-into-empty; gzip+brotli only with the zstd C-dependency exclusion verified) is sealed doctrine; future server stories cite it rather than re-litigating.

Deliberately-lexical go-to-definition is argued from the user's experience (mid-keystroke documents don't parse; a feature that only works on valid documents reads as broken); None-over-guessing posture everywhere (unbalanced brackets, unknown hover, no dbPath guessing from rootUri — silently creating a store nobody asked for is the worse failure mode).

ban: Do not keep or re-mint an engine-side lsp_api door.
Do not twin LineIndex between main.rs and translate.rs.
Do not add a local formatter to the LSP (kyzo-model format.rs is the one pretty-printer).
Do not guess dbPath from rootUri.
Do not let the LSP hand-roll message SHAPES (lsp-types stays; only the transport framing is hand-rolled, by ruling).
Do not reshape crates/kyzo-core/tests/ to chase facade drift — the suite breaking IS the signal.
Do not remove the per-route body limit or widen it router-wide.

rewire: TARGET kyzo-lsp/src/translate.rs ← LineIndex + diagnostics translation from kyzo-lsp/src/main.rs (intra-crate move, no condemned file)
TARGET kyzo-lsp/src/main.rs ← validation calls kyzo-model public parse surface (lsp_api door dies on kyzo-core/src/lib.rs)

proof_meters: compile-fail: host crates cannot reach non-re-exported kyzo-core internals (public_api_surface suite locks the export set)

crates/kyzo-lsp/src/translate.rs.kind: TARGET — miette → LSP: spans to ranges, errors to diagnostics
ban: guessing on unmappable positions (clamp, never crash; None, never guess)
carried_notes: LSP positions are UTF-16 CODE UNITS — the rationale names exactly which diagnostics would render at the wrong column (escape/codepoint diagnostics carrying non-ASCII spans); position CLAMPS past-the-end; offset is the honest inverse with the spec's own over-long-position rule.

One Diagnostic per label across the error tree — the same shape parse fuzz's walk_labels proves every parser error satisfies, walked here from the public Diagnostic trait; Display + #[help] in one message so the mechanical fix shows up in the editor; defensive no-label fallback with its reason.
validate publishes empty-on-success because clearing previously-published diagnostics is as important as reporting new ones.

crates/kyzo-lsp/src/main.rs.kind: TARGET living seat — transport, server loop, features, catalog tier
ban: hand-rolled message shapes (lsp-types only; transport framing exempt by ruling)
carried_notes: Transport ruling: hand-rolled Content-Length framing, no async runtime — but every request/notification param is lsp-types, the same crate rust-analyzer uses; clean-EOF vs mid-message-EOF distinguished so a truncated stream is diagnosable.

Catalog scope: dbPath ONLY, opening a real store so hover/completion answer from the connected catalog, not a shadow; degrades catalog-free without one; an unparseable URI is the client's bug, not ours to crash over.
didClose clears stale squiggles; full-sync-only invariant stated at didOpen/didChange.

crates/kyzo-core/src/lib.rs.kind: TARGET living seat — sealed facade; the lsp_api door dies here
ban: single-host doors on the sealed facade

crates/kyzo-bin/src/server/mod.rs.kind: TARGET living seat — route table + state + startup; ledger sealed
carried_notes: internal_error handles JoinError only — everything the engine itself can refuse comes back as Ok(Err(_)).
Startup unwraps are process-entry failures, lawful under the zone's no-panic-escapes-a-HANDLER clause; the bind/port parse lift to a typed refusal is the seat's recorded next edit (refused.items).

must_add: kyzo-bin/src/engine.rs note: 'opens and holds a live Db' becomes 'holds the Engine composition' when the storage epic executes §1 (cross-reference 07)
delete_meter: kyzo-lsp/src/translate.rs remains on the map with its miette→LSP description
kyzo-lsp and kyzo-bin server entries cite docs/deprecated/storage-model/06-hosts-lsp.json

final_meters: kyzo-lsp/src/translate.rs exceeds stub length; LineIndex defined exactly once, in translate.rs
crates/kyzo-core/tests/ suite green against the facade after the door deletion

seated_laws.law: clamp-never-crash, None-never-guess — the LSP's posture on hostile/degenerate input
seated_laws.meter: rg -n 'panic!|unwrap\(|expect\(' crates/kyzo-lsp/src → zero on request-handling paths outside cfg(test)
exceptions: process-entry startup failures (transport stdin/stdout acquisition) — lawful under the no-panic-escapes-a-HANDLER clause, each justified at the site

qae.q: zone-lsp forbids depending on the engine ('the language server needs the language, not the database'), yet the shipped catalog features (hover columns, relation completion) open a real fjall Db and run ::relations/::columns through the session tier. The census refused to choose. Rule it: do the catalog features move out, or does the law gain a sanctioned door?
qae.a: Keep the catalog features; amend zone-lsp to forbid engine INTERNALS, not the sealed public door. The LSP may consume exactly the same sealed kyzo:: facade any host (kyzo-bin) consumes, read-only, dbPath-explicit — and nothing else. A separately-minted read-only introspection crate would be a second door for one host; moving the features out deletes shipped user value to satisfy a law whose intent (no engine coupling past the seal) the sealed door already honors.
qae.e: The engine-internal coupling the zone law fears is real; the sealed facade is precisely the boundary built to make such consumption safe — hosts import only what lib.rs re-exports (compile-fail proof). Close: zone-lsp text amendment (operator edit); meter — kyzo-lsp imports only kyzo (facade) + kyzo-model + lsp-types; rg for any non-facade kyzo-core path → zero. Recorded as cut_destiny; the zone-law text edit itself is the operator's pen.

qae.q: Full go-to-definition and completion want fixed-rule and aggregation vocabulary. 05 puts name+arity declarations in kyzo-model. Is that the whole discharge, or does the LSP still need an engine edge for live registry contents?
qae.a: Model vocabulary is the whole discharge for language features: names, arities, keywords come from kyzo-model (killing the three hand-kept lists). Live registry contents (which rules THIS store's engine actually has loaded) are catalog-tier questions and flow through the same sealed-door catalog channel as ::relations — never a new edge.
qae.e: Splitting language-static from store-live keeps the language server honest: static answers never misreport what the engine accepts (the drift-honesty note's blast radius), live answers come from the connected store or degrade to None. Close: carried_obligations.lsp-consts-become-model-vocabulary; meter — AGGREGATIONS/KEYWORDS consts deleted from main.rs once model vocabulary lands.
